hero.title: Bulk import scientific references from a list of URLs
hero.subtitle: Feed Zotero a plain text file of URLs. Metadata is fetched through Zotero translators — exactly like the browser extension, just in bulk.
hero.imageAlt: Stack of glowing documents flowing into a Zotero library

features.title: Everything you need for batch imports
features.subtitle: Built for researchers handling long reading lists.

collection.title: Target collection
collection.desc: Hierarchical dropdown of every library, collection and sub-collection.

local.title: 100% local
local.desc: Everything runs inside your Zotero. No data leaves your machine, no third-party server.

delay.title: Configurable delay
delay.desc: Adjust the wait time between requests (default: 3s) to stay polite.

progress.title: Live progress & log
progress.desc: Real-time progress bar and a journal of every URL processed.

pdf.title: PDF auto-attach
pdf.desc: When a translator provides one, the PDF is downloaded and attached to the reference automatically.

fallback.title: Smart fallback
fallback.desc: If no Zotero translator matches, the page is saved as a web snapshot.

how.title: How it works
how.subtitle: Three steps from a list of links to a tidy collection.

one.title: Prepare a .txt
one.desc: One URL per line. Empty lines are ignored.

two.title: Pick a collection
two.desc: Select a target library and collection in Zotero.

three.title: Launch the import
three.desc: Watch the progress bar — stop or resume any time.

format.title: Input format
format.subtitle: A plain text file. One URL per line. That's it.

install.title: Installation
install.subtitle: Two paths: install the packaged plugin, or load it in dev mode.

xpi.step3: Click the gear icon, choose "Install Add-on From File…"

dev.step2: In your Zotero profile directory, open the extensions/ folder
dev.step3: Create a file named bulk-import@atlasfieldREPLACE (no extension) containing the absolute path to this repo
dev.step4: Remove extensions.lastAppBuildId and extensions.lastAppVersion from prefs.js, then relaunch Zotero

compat.title: Compatibility
compat.subtitle: Designed for the modern Zotero stack.

q1.a: The supported sites are exactly the same as those you can save with the Zotero Connector browser extension.

q2.q: What if no translator matches a URL?
q2.a: The plugin falls back to saving the page as a web snapshot, so you never lose an entry.

q3.a: To avoid overloading the publishers' servers, a configurable delay is applied between each request (default: 3 seconds).

q5.a: No. Everything runs locally in your Zotero instance using the standard translators.
